Major Cosmic Eras.description: Before the first stars — neutral hydrogen and helium, no light sources.
The first massive stars (Population III) reionize the intergalactic gas.
Peak activity of supermassive black holes, intense star and galaxy formation.
Stabilized galaxies, slowed star formation, expansion accelerated by dark energy.

Major Cosmic Eras.titre: From the Primordial Universe to Earth
Major Cosmic Eras.description: Primordial nucleosynthesis, formation of galaxies, the Sun and the solar system.

Milky Way & Solar System.description: Our galaxy forms through successive mergers of proto-galaxies.
Stars rich in heavy metals from the first supernovae.
Collapse of a molecular cloud forming the Sun and its planets.
From the first organic molecules to complex life forms.

Cosmology.description: Origin of space, time and matter — 13.8 billion years ago.
380,000 years after the Big Bang: the universe cools enough for atoms to form and light to travel freely.
First massive stars (Population III) — synthesis of the first heavy elements.
Proto-galaxies merge under the force of gravity.
Our galaxy takes shape through accretion and successive mergers.

Solar System.description: Collapse of a cloud of gas and dust — birth of our star.
Accretion of planetesimals around the young Sun.
Collision of Earth with Theia — ejection of matter forming the Moon.
First self-replicating molecules in the primitive oceans.
Impact of the Chicxulub asteroid — ecological niches freed for mammals.
Our species emerges in Africa — the latest great innovation in the observable universe.
13.8 billion years after the Big Bang.
